prompt: Translate this English proverb into two other languages in a way that preserves the spirit, not the words: 'The best time to plant a tree was twenty years ago. The second best time is now.' Add a one-sentence note on each.
response:
Here are the translations:

**French:** "L'occasion idéale pour planter un arbre, c'est souvent lorsque l'on n'y pense pas ; mais il n'est jamais trop tard pour commencer." 
Note: This translation preserves the spirit of the original proverb by using a similar structure and conveying the idea that while the ideal time may have passed, now is still a good time to take action.

**Spanish:** "Aunque el mejor momento para sembrar un árbol fue hace mucho tiempo, cada día es una oportunidad para dar el primer paso hacia un futuro mejor." 
Note: This translation maintains the essence of the original proverb by emphasizing that while the optimal time may have passed, every day presents a new chance to start working towards a better future.

These translations aim to preserve the spirit of the original proverb while using language and structures that are natural in the target languages.
